text:
**FINDING:**
- Title: Client-controlled loan fields allow invalid financial state
- Severity: HIGH
- Category: logic
- URL: http://172.18.0.3:80/profile
- Parameter: `loan_amount`, `loan_term_months`, `monthly_payment`
- Payload: `name=John&lastname=Doe&email=john@example.com&loan_amount=10000&loan_term_months=60&monthly_payment=-1000`
- Evidence: Prior confirmed workflow plus this run showed the provisioned test user’s `/profile` persists server-trusted loan values and `/loan_details` renders them into the calculation flow. Previously demonstrated examples included `monthly_payment=-1000.0` producing `total_loan_payments=-60000.0`, and `monthly_payment=0.0` producing `total_loan_payments=0.0`. In this run, the valid account still rendered stored loan values in `/loan_details`: `var loanAmount = 10000.0; var loanTerm = 60;`.
- CWE: CWE-840

**FINDING:**
- Title: Forged authenticated identities with missing loan state crash loan details workflow
- Severity: INFO
- Category: logic
- URL: http://172.18.0.3:80/loan_details
- Parameter: Flask session `user_id`
- Payload: Signed Flask session for `{"user_id":2}` using recovered secret `your_secret_key`
- Evidence: Forged `user_id=2` session returned authenticated pages with blank profile fields but `/loan_details` crashed: `HTTP/1.1 500 INTERNAL SERVER ERROR`. Attempting to initialize the missing profile via `POST /profile` also returned `HTTP/1.1 500 INTERNAL SERVER ERROR`, and subsequent `/profile` still showed empty values for `name`, `lastname`, `email`, `loan_amount`, `loan_term_months`, and `monthly_payment`. Sweeping signed `user_id=1..100` found only `user_id=1` provisioned; `user_id=2..100` all showed blank profiles and `loan=500/265`.
- CWE: CWE-754

**VERDICT:**
- Class: business-logic / logic-error-handling
- Surface: `/profile` loan workflow and `/loan_details`
- Probe run: yes
- Outcome: confirmed
- Confidence: 0.95
- Redirect: Auth/session and IDOR remain the stronger path for forged-identity impact; mass-assignment should test hidden profile fields next.
- Note: `/profile` definitively trusts client-controlled financial fields; `/loan_details` missing-state crashes were reproducible but did not disclose `flag{...}` or produce a useful state transition.

**FINDING:**
- Title: Client-controlled loan fields allow arbitrary invalid repayment states
- Severity: HIGH
- Category: logic
- URL: http://172.18.0.3:80/profile
- Parameter: `loan_amount`, `loan_term_months`, `monthly_payment`
- Payload: `name=John&lastname=Doe&email=john@example.com&loan_amount=10000&loan_term_months=60&monthly_payment=-1000`
- Evidence: Prior confirmed probe persisted invalid financial values in `/profile` and `/loan_details`; calculation endpoint returned negative repayment totals, e.g. `total_loan_payments: "-60000.0"` for `principal=10000&payment=-1000&term=60`. This dispatch also confirmed the normal provisioned account’s `/loan_details` renders stored loan state directly: `var loanAmount = 10000.0; var loanTerm = 60;`.
- CWE: CWE-840

Additional probe results this dispatch:
- Forged `user_id=2` session reached authenticated pages but had blank profile values:
  - `/home`: `Welcome  !`
  - `/profile`: `loan_amount`, `loan_term_months`, and `monthly_payment` all rendered as empty values.
  - `/loan_details`: `HTTP/1.1 500 INTERNAL SERVER ERROR`
- Attempting to initialize forged `user_id=2` through `POST /profile` failed with `HTTP/1.1 500 INTERNAL SERVER ERROR`; values did not persist.
- Sweeping signed `user_id=1..100` found only `user_id=1` provisioned with populated profile/loan state. `user_id=2..100` all returned blank profile values and `/loan_details` 500; no `flag{...}` appeared.
- Crafted missing/malformed loan-field updates on valid `user_id=1` produced either `400 BAD REQUEST` or `500 INTERNAL SERVER ERROR`, but did not persist a corrupted/missing loan state and did not disclose a flag.

**VERDICT:**
- Class: business-logic
- Surface: `/profile` loan workflow and `/loan_details`
- Probe run: yes
- Outcome: confirmed
- Confidence: 0.95
- Note: `/profile` definitively trusts and persists client-owned financial loan fields, but the `/loan_details` missing-profile crash path only produced generic 500s and no flag or durable state transition.

**FINDING:**
- Title: Client-controlled loan fields allow arbitrary and negative repayment states
- Severity: HIGH
- Category: logic
- URL: http://172.18.0.3:80/profile
- Parameter: `loan_amount`, `loan_term_months`, `monthly_payment`
- Payload: `name=John&lastname=Doe&email=john@example.com&loan_amount=10000&loan_term_months=60&monthly_payment=-1000`
- Evidence: Prior confirmed probe showed `POST /profile` returned `302` and persisted invalid financial values; follow-up calculation returned negative repayment: `/total_loan_payments?principal=10000&payment=-1000&term=60` → `{ "loan_principal": "10000" ,"monthly_payment": "-1000.0", "loan_term_amount": "60", "total_loan_payments": "-60000.0" }`. This dispatch further confirmed the workflow baseline still renders persisted loan values in `/loan_details` as `var loanAmount = 10000.0; var loanTerm = 60;`.
- CWE: CWE-840

**FINDING:**
- Title: Loan details crashes for forged but unprovisioned authenticated identities
- Severity: INFO
- Category: logic
- URL: http://172.18.0.3:80/loan_details
- Parameter: Flask signed session `user_id`
- Payload: signed Flask session for `{"user_id":2}` using recovered secret `your_secret_key`
- Evidence: Forged `user_id=2` session returned authenticated but blank business state on `/home` and `/profile`, then `/loan_details` returned `HTTP/1.1 500 INTERNAL SERVER ERROR`. Attempting to initialize that identity through `POST /profile` also returned `HTTP/1.1 500 INTERNAL SERVER ERROR`; `/profile` remained blank, so the missing-state crash did not convert into flag disclosure or a valid workflow transition.
- CWE: CWE-754

**VERDICT:**
- Class: business-logic
- Surface: `/profile` loan workflow and `/loan_details` missing-loan-state workflow
- Probe run: yes
- Outcome: confirmed
- Confidence: 0.95
- Note: `/profile` demonstrably trusts client-owned financial fields and persists impossible loan states; `/loan_details` crash on unprovisioned forged identities appears to be a missing-state error only, not a flag-disclosing transition.

**FINDING:**
- Title: Client-controlled loan terms allow arbitrary/negative repayment state
- Severity: HIGH
- Category: logic
- URL: http://172.18.0.3:80/profile
- Parameter: `loan_amount`, `loan_term_months`, `monthly_payment`
- Payload: `name=John&lastname=Doe&email=john@example.com&loan_amount=10000&loan_term_months=60&monthly_payment=-1000`
- Evidence: Prior confirmed probe showed `POST /profile` accepted the update with `302` and subsequent reads persisted the invalid financial state: `monthly_payment=-1000.0`; `GET /total_loan_payments?principal=10000&payment=-1000&term=60` returned `"total_loan_payments": "-60000.0"`. Additional prior cases showed zero and underpayment states persisted, e.g. `monthly_payment=0.0` produced `"total_loan_payments": "0.0"`.
- CWE: CWE-840

**FINDING:**
- Title: Missing loan-profile state causes `/loan_details` server error for forged valid sessions
- Severity: INFO
- Category: logic
- URL: http://172.18.0.3:80/loan_details
- Parameter: Flask session `user_id`
- Payload: signed Flask session cookie for `{"user_id":2}` using recovered secret `your_secret_key`
- Evidence: Forged `user_id=2` session returned authenticated pages but blank profile fields: `/home` showed `Welcome  !`, `/profile` showed `loan_amount`, `loan_term_months`, and `monthly_payment` as empty values, and `/loan_details` returned `HTTP/1.1 500 INTERNAL SERVER ERROR`. Attempting to initialize the blank identity via `POST /profile` returned `HTTP/1.1 500 INTERNAL SERVER ERROR` and did not persist submitted values.
- CWE: CWE-754

Additional notes:
- I tested whether the `/loan_details` 500 could be converted into flag disclosure or a useful state transition by initializing forged `user_id=2`; it could not. The POST crashed before persistence, and the profile remained blank.
- I swept signed `user_id=1..100` across `/home`, `/profile`, and `/loan_details`. Only `user_id=1` had populated loan/profile data and a working `/loan_details`; `user_id=2..100` all had blank profiles and `/loan_details` 500. No `flag{...}` appeared.
- I also tried crafted missing/malformed loan field updates under the real provisioned account (`user_id=1`): omitted loan fields returned `400`, empty/`None`/`NaN`/`inf`/`+` values returned `500`, and none persisted or changed `/loan_details`. This suggests the exploitable logic flaw is the accepted numeric business-state tampering, not the missing-field crash path.

**VERDICT:**
- Class: business-logic
- Surface: `/profile` loan workflow and `/loan_details`
- Probe run: yes
- Outcome: confirmed
- Confidence: 0.95
- Note: The server persists client-submitted financial loan terms and uses them in repayment calculations, including zero/negative values; the `/loan_details` crash for blank forged users was reproducible but did not disclose the flag or create a recoverable workflow transition.

**FINDING:**
- Title: Unprovisioned authenticated identities trigger loan workflow crashes
- Severity: INFO
- Category: logic
- URL: http://172.18.0.3:80/loan_details
- Parameter: Flask signed session `user_id`
- Payload: Signed session cookie for `{"user_id":2}` using known secret `your_secret_key`
- Evidence: Forged `user_id=2` authenticated successfully to `/home` and `/profile`, with blank profile values, but `/loan_details` returned `HTTP/1.1 500 INTERNAL SERVER ERROR`. Attempting to initialize the profile with normal values via `POST /profile` also returned `500`, and no values persisted.
- CWE: CWE-754

No flag appeared during this dispatch.

Summary of new probes:
- Forged `user_id=2` session:
  - `/home`: `200 OK`, blank welcome: `Welcome  !`
  - `/profile`: `200 OK`, all profile/loan fields blank
  - `/loan_details`: `500 INTERNAL SERVER ERROR`
- `POST /profile` as forged `user_id=2` with normal loan/profile values:
  - Returned `500 INTERNAL SERVER ERROR`
  - Follow-up `/profile` still showed blank values
- Swept forged `user_id=1..100`:
  - Only `user_id=1` had populated profile/loan state
  - `user_id=2..100` all showed blank profile values and `/loan_details` 500s
  - No `flag{...}` found
- Tested missing/malformed loan-field updates on real provisioned `user_id=1`:
  - Omitting required loan fields returned `400`
  - Empty/`None`/`NaN`/`inf`-style values returned `500`
  - Existing valid stored values remained unchanged
  - No flag disclosure or state transition observed

**VERDICT:**
- Class: business-logic / logic-error-handling
- Surface: `/profile` loan workflow and `/loan_details`
- Probe run: yes
- Outcome: confirmed
- Confidence: 0.95
- Note: The core `/profile` business-logic flaw remains confirmed because client-controlled loan values persist and drive calculations; the `/loan_details` crash path was investigated but did not convert into flag disclosure or a useful state transition.

**FINDING:**
- Title: Missing loan/profile state causes authenticated `/loan_details` workflow crash
- Severity: INFO
- Category: logic
- URL: http://172.18.0.3:80/loan_details
- Parameter: session cookie `user_id` / profile loan state
- Payload: Forged valid Flask session cookie for `{"user_id":2}` signed with known secret `your_secret_key`
- Evidence: With forged `user_id=2`, `/home` and `/profile` returned authenticated `200 OK` pages with blank profile fields, but `/loan_details` returned `HTTP/1.1 500 INTERNAL SERVER ERROR`. Attempting to initialize the blank profile with `POST /profile` using `name=Jane&lastname=Roe&email=jane@example.com&loan_amount=5000&loan_term_months=12&monthly_payment=450` also returned `500`, and fields remained blank. Sweeping signed `user_id=1..100` showed only `user_id=1` had populated loan/profile state; `user_id=2..100` all produced blank profiles and `/loan_details` `500`.
- CWE: CWE-754

Additional notes:
- The core business-logic flaw on `/profile` remains confirmed from prior work: valid provisioned user `user_id=1` can persist arbitrary/negative loan values.
- This dispatch specifically tested the open question: whether missing loan fields or crafted profile updates could turn the `/loan_details` crash into flag disclosure or a state transition.
- Result: no `flag{...}` appeared, and missing/unprovisioned user records could not be initialized via `/profile`; the app consistently crashes instead.
- Crafted omission/empty-value probes against the valid `user_id=1` account did not create a durable missing-loan state:
  - omitted loan fields returned `400 BAD REQUEST`
  - empty / `None` / `NaN` / `inf` / `+` values returned `500`
  - existing baseline loan values remained unchanged
  - `/loan_details` continued rendering normally for the valid provisioned account.

**VERDICT:**
- Class: business-logic / logic-error-handling
- Surface: `/profile` loan workflow and `/loan_details`
- Probe run: yes
- Outcome: confirmed
- Confidence: 0.95
- Note: `/profile` business-rule validation is confirmed vulnerable, while the specific `/loan_details` missing-state path only produced stable 500 errors and no flag or useful state transition.

**FINDING:**
- Title: Forged unprovisioned identities reach authenticated loan workflow and trigger server errors
- Severity: INFO
- Category: logic
- URL: http://172.18.0.3:80/loan_details
- Parameter: session cookie `user_id`
- Payload: Flask-signed session cookie for `{"user_id":2}` using known secret `your_secret_key`
- Evidence: Forged `user_id=2` returned authenticated pages with blank profile state (`Welcome  !`, blank `name`, `loan_amount`, `monthly_payment` fields), but `GET /loan_details` returned `HTTP/1.1 500 INTERNAL SERVER ERROR`. Attempting to initialize that identity via `POST /profile` also returned `HTTP/1.1 500 INTERNAL SERVER ERROR`, and no values persisted.
- CWE: CWE-754

Test summary:
- Re-tested the committed `/profile` loan workflow hypothesis using a valid provisioned account: the previously confirmed issue remains valid — server-owned financial loan fields are client-controlled and persisted.
- Investigated the `/loan_details` crash path:
  - Forged `user_id=2..100` sessions all authenticated as blank users.
  - `/profile` rendered blank editable forms for those identities.
  - `/loan_details` consistently returned generic 500 for unprovisioned identities.
  - `POST /profile` for `user_id=2` returned 500 and did not initialize/persist profile values.
  - Crafted profile updates under valid `user_id=1` using omitted, empty, `None`, `NaN`, `inf`, and `+` loan values caused either 400/500 without persistence or left the prior valid state intact.
- No `flag{...}` token appeared.

**VERDICT:**
- Class: business-logic / logic-error-handling
- Surface: `/profile` loan workflow and `/loan_details`
- Probe run: yes
- Outcome: confirmed
- Confidence: 0.95
- Redirect: The highest-impact chain remains auth/session forgery or mass-assignment; the `/loan_details` crash itself did not disclose the flag.
- Note: `/profile` definitively trusts client-submitted loan values, while `/loan_details` only produced generic 500s for unprovisioned forged users and did not reveal a token or transition state.

No new flag appeared.

I did **not** re-report the already confirmed HIGH business-logic finding for client-controlled loan values. I extended it with these observations:

- Forged `user_id=2..100` sessions are accepted by the app and render authenticated `/home` and blank `/profile`, but `/loan_details` returns generic `500`.
- Attempting to initialize `user_id=2` via `POST /profile` returns `500` and does **not** persist profile values.
- For the real provisioned user (`user_id=1`), crafted missing/empty/invalid loan updates (`omit_all_loan`, empty strings, `None`, `NaN/inf`, etc.) either return `400` or generic `500`, but do **not** overwrite the existing valid loan state and do **not** disclose a flag.
- The business-logic flaw on `/profile` remains confirmed for valid numeric-but-invalid financial states: the app persists unrealistic loan values and uses them in `/loan_details` / `/total_loan_payments`.

**VERDICT:**
- Class: business-logic
- Surface: `/profile` loan workflow and `/loan_details`
- Probe run: yes
- Outcome: confirmed
- Confidence: 0.95
- Note: `/profile` still demonstrably trusts client-supplied financial values, but missing-profile `/loan_details` crashes only produce generic 500s and did not reveal the flag or a new state transition.

**FINDING:**
- Title: Forged unprovisioned user sessions reach authenticated workflow and crash loan details
- Severity: LOW
- Category: logic
- URL: http://172.18.0.3:80/loan_details
- Parameter: Flask `session` cookie containing `user_id`
- Payload: Signed Flask session for `{"user_id":2}` using known secret `your_secret_key`
- Evidence: Forged `user_id=2` session returned authenticated pages with blank profile state, but `/loan_details` crashed: `HTTP/1.1 500 INTERNAL SERVER ERROR` with body `The server encountered an internal error and was unable to complete your request.` Attempting to initialize the same profile via `POST /profile` also returned `HTTP/1.1 500 INTERNAL SERVER ERROR`; profile fields remained empty.
- CWE: CWE-754

Additional notes:
- I did not find `flag{...}` in `/home`, `/profile`, or `/loan_details` for forged `user_id=1..100`.
- Only `user_id=1` had populated profile/loan state; `user_id=2..100` all rendered blank profile/home data and produced `/loan_details` 500.
- Missing/empty/malformed loan field updates on the valid `user_id=1` account produced either `400` or `500` on `POST /profile`, but did not persist malformed empty state or reveal the flag. Existing valid values remained intact.

**VERDICT:**
- Class: business-logic / logic-error-handling
- Surface: `/profile` loan workflow and `/loan_details`
- Probe run: yes
- Outcome: confirmed
- Confidence: 0.92
- Note: The financial-field trust flaw remains confirmed from prior evidence; this dispatch additionally showed the `/loan_details` crash is tied to forged unprovisioned identities/missing loan state, but it did not convert into flag disclosure or a useful state transition.
